1.11a: Underwriting Department: PO Box 17225, Solihull, B91 9US
1.11b: Contact Underwriting Department as per section 1.11a
1.7: The lender will not provide loans where the borrower and mortgagor are not the same entity.
1.14: Conveyancers may not act if the transaction handler or their immediate family is the seller.
1.15: Conveyancers may act if the handler or family is the borrower only if the firm is a partnership.
A non-related partner must handle the transaction.
No conflict of interest must exist.
Details must be submitted in writing and express authority received from the lender before starting work.
3.1.3: Seller's solicitor details are only required upon specific request.
3.1.4: Lender notification is not required if the seller is unrepresented.
Conveyancers must ensure security is protected and follow professional body guidance.
Undertakings from unqualified conveyancers are prohibited.
3.1.5: Latest pension statement is an acceptable identity document from List B.
At least one identity document must contain the borrower's authenticated signature.
3.2.3: Seller's solicitor details are only required upon specific request.
3.2.4: Use contact details provided in section 1.11a.
4.5a: Use contact details provided in section 1.11a for re-inspections.
4.5b: Send Certificate of Title to the Completions Department.
4.1: Lender provides a valuation report for every transaction.
4.3: Use contact details provided in section 1.11a to report incorrect valuation assumptions.
5.1.1: Purchases: Seller must have owned the property for at least 6 months, unless it is a developer part-exchange.
Remortgages: Borrower must have owned the property for at least 6 months, unless acquired via inheritance.
5.2.1: Use contact details provided in section 1.11a if the seller is not the registered proprietor.
5.4.4: Environmental and contaminated land reports must be submitted.
5.4.5: Personal searches are accepted if the agency has adequate professional indemnity insurance.
The conveyancer must be able to provide an unqualified certificate of title.
5.4.6: Search insurance is accepted at the conveyancer's risk.
Title must remain good and marketable.
5.5.3a: Use contact details provided in section 1.11a to report breaches or conditions that will not be met by completion.
5.5.3b: Original or copy of planning permission required after completion.
Lending is prohibited on Grade 1 (England/Wales) and Grade A (Scotland) listed properties.
5.5.3c: Building regulation consents required after completion.
5.5.3d: Certificates of lawful use or development required after completion for new builds, extensions/alterations within last 4 years, or material change of use within last 10 years.
5.5.4: Report restrictions affecting marketability to the contact in section 1.11a.
5.7.1a: Flying freeholds accepted if they comprise less than 15% of the total floor space.
Conveyancer must verify rights of support, access, and maintenance.
Report flying freeholds to lender if they are not mentioned in the valuation report.
5.7.1b: Lending on freehold flats is prohibited.
5.7.1c: Report accepted flying freeholds to the contact in section 1.11a.
5.8.1: Security involving building conversions of max 4 flats is accepted if borrower occupies one flat and owns/shares the freehold, with other flats on long leases.
5.8.5: Two-flat leasehold arrangements accepted if lender takes security over both the borrower's leasehold flat and the freehold interest in the other flat.
5.10.1: Report use restrictions to the contact in section 1.11a.
5.13.1: Report balance of purchase price issues to the contact in section 1.11a.
5.14.15a: Report absentee or insolvent landlords to the contact in section 1.11a.
5.14.15b: Indemnity insurance for absent/insolvent landlords requires Underwriting Department referral.
Insurance must comply with Section 9 and ensure good/marketable title.
5.14.1: Leasehold remaining term plus age of the youngest borrower at completion must be 175 years or greater.
Transfer fees must be fixed, capped, or calculable.
Transfer fees must not exceed 3% of the property's market value or sale price.
5.14.9: Lease matters contact: Legal Services Department.
5.14.10: Maximum total annual service charge (including ground rent and sinking fund) is 2.5% of the property value.
5.14.11: Indemnity insurance for unsatisfactory lease terms requires Underwriting Department referral.
5.14.12: Send ground rent and service charge receipts after completion.
5.14.13: Send receipted notice or evidence of service after completion.
5.15.2a: Management company issues contact: Underwriting Department (1.11a).
5.15.2b: Send management company share certificate after completion.
5.15.2c: Send signed blank stock transfer form after completion.
5.15.2d: Management company memorandum and articles of association are not required.
5.16.2: Report search entry certification issues to the contact in 1.11a.
5.17.5a: Solicitor-certified copy of Power of Attorney required.
Lifetime mortgages: Attorney can only sign if borrower is incapacitated and the power is an EPA or LPA.
Must confirm mortgage is for the donor's welfare and benefit.
Clear bankruptcy search required for all attorneys.
5.17.5b: Statutory declaration of non-revocation of power of attorney required.
5.19.1: Lending on shared ownership, shared equity, or affordable housing is prohibited.
5.20.1: Report solar PV panel leases that do not meet UK Finance minimum requirements.
5.20.3: Solar PV roof space leases must comply with the lender's specific requirements.
5.20.4: Disclose existing Green Deal Plans to the Underwriting Department.
6.1.3: Report non-uptake of mortgage offer to the contact in 1.11a.
6.2.1: Report property description discrepancies to the contact in 1.11a.
6.3.1: Report purchase price issues to the contact in 1.11a.
6.4.4: All incentives must be reported unless explicitly detailed in the valuation report.
6.4.5: Report lack of control over purchase money to the contact in 1.11a.
6.5.1: Report vacant possession issues to the contact in 1.11a.
6.6.1: Report letting/buy-to-let status to the contact in 1.11a.
6.6.2: Report missing letting details to the contact in 1.11a.
6.6.4: Lending on Houses in Multiple Occupation (HMOs) is prohibited.
6.7.1: Accepted warranty schemes: NHBC, Premier Guarantee (LABC), Building Life Plans, Checkmate, ICW, Homeproof, Q Assure Build, Advantage HCI, Buildzone, Protek, Ark, One Guarantee, Global Home Warranties, ABC+ Warranty, Build Assure (FMB), Cadis.
Other schemes require referral to Underwriting Department.
6.7.3: Send building standards indemnity scheme assignments after completion.
6.7.4: A new home warranty scheme is mandatory for new properties.
6.7.6: Professional consultant's certificate required only if specifically approved.
6.8.1: Report missing road/sewer agreements or bonds to the contact in 1.11a.
6.9.1: Report missing easements to the contact in 1.11a.
6.11.1: Report redevelopment or road proposals to the contact in 1.11a.
6.12.1: Report pre-emption rights or resale restrictions to the contact in 1.11a.
6.13.1: Report undischarged improvement/repair grants to the contact in 1.11a.
7.3: Consent to mortgage required from all occupants aged 17 and over.
7.4: Report doubts regarding information accuracy to the contact in 1.11a.
8.1: Conveyancers may not advise the specified third parties.
9.1: Send indemnity insurance policy after completion.
9.2: Indemnity limit must equal full market value and be index-linked.
Policy must benefit the lender, the borrower (where available), and all successors/assigns.
10.2a: A fee of £30 is applicable for CHAPS payments.
10.2b: Minimum 5 working days' notice required for funds.
10.3: Standard deductions are listed in the Offer of Loan.
10.7: Return delayed advances to the contact in 1.11a.
10.9: Report delayed completion to the contact in 1.11a.
10.10: Lender must be notified immediately if completion is delayed.
Full advance must be returned via CHAPS without deduction within one business day.
Return remittance must quote borrower name and account number.
10.11: Interest for late return of funds is charged at the lender's funding cost rate.
12.3.1: Retention and instalment release contact: Underwriting Department (1.11a).
14.1.4: Form CH2 application is not applicable.
14.1.5: Send original mortgage deed and original title documents after completion.
14.2.1: Send all deeds and documents to the Administration Department.
14.2.2: Post-completion document list: Certificate of Title, Title information document, original legal charge, original/certified lease, Land Registry/Charges searches, original certificates, original indemnity policies, receipted notices.
16.3.7a: Transfer of equity execution contact: Administration Department.
16.3.7b: Standard attestation: 'Executed as a Deed by [Name] as Director of [Company] in presence of Witness [Name]'
16.3.1: No standard form for transfer or deed of covenant.
16.3.4: Send certified copy of transfer of equity after completion.
16.4.1: Consent to letting contact: Administration Department.
16.4.2: Copy of proposed tenancy agreement must be submitted.
16.5.3a: Deed of variation destination: Administration Department.
16.5.3b: Deed of rectification destination: Administration Department.
16.5.3c: Deed of easement destination: Administration Department.
16.5.2: Confirm impact of deeds/agreements with the Administration Department.
17.2.1a: Discharge and repayment remittance destination: Administration Department.
17.2.1b: Mortgage discharge is processed electronically via e-DS1.
